# ADR-002: Use Newtonsoft.Json for Serialization

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The SDK serializes and deserializes complex Contentful JSON payloads including polymorphic field types, rich text nodes, dynamic `Entry<T>` generics, and `JToken`-based helpers. A serialization library must support:

- Custom converters (for Contentful-specific type discrimination)
- `JObject`/`JToken` manipulation for schema-agnostic patterns
- `netstandard2.0` support

When the SDK was first authored (pre-2017), `System.Text.Json` did not exist (it was introduced in .NET Core 3.0). `Newtonsoft.Json` was the de-facto standard for all .NET JSON work.

## Decision

Use `Newtonsoft.Json` (currently pinned at 13.0.1) as the sole JSON library. The public API surface exposes `JToken`/`JObject` types in extension methods and helper utilities, making it part of the public contract.

## Consequences

- **Enables:** Full Newtonsoft feature set — custom converters, `JObject` manipulation, `[JsonProperty]` attributes, LINQ-to-JSON.
- **Prevents:** Transparent migration to `System.Text.Json` — that would be a breaking public API change since `JToken`/`JObject` are Newtonsoft types.
- **Trade-off accepted:** Adds a dependency that Microsoft considers "legacy" for new .NET projects, but it is actively maintained and widely used. Pin version to avoid transitive conflicts.
- **Follow-up:** A future v2 major version could offer a `System.Text.Json` variant or abstraction layer. Track as tech debt.
